heading: Reductions and other adjustments in force
label: 1610 Reductions and other adjustments in force
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1635 of Pub. L. 104–201section 1593 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1635 of Pub. L. 104–201section 1593 of this title

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall prescribe regulations for the separation of employees in defense intelligence positions, including members of the Defense Intelligence Senior Executive Service and employees in Intelligence Senior Level positions, during a reduction in force or other adjustment in force. The regulations shall apply to such a reduction in force or other adjustment in force notwithstanding sections 3501(b) and 3502 of title 5.
Military preference, subject to sections 3501(a)(3) and 3502(b) of title 5.
The veteran’s preference under .section 3502(b) of title 5
Length of service computed in accordance with the second sentence of .section 3502(a) of title 5

children.heading: .—Regulations Relating to Defense Intelligence SES
children.label: c .—Regulations Relating to Defense Intelligence SES
children.text: The regulations relating to removal from the Defense Intelligence Senior Executive Service in a reduction in force or other adjustment in force shall be consistent with .section 3595(a) of title 5

The regulations shall provide a right of appeal regarding a personnel action under the regulations. The appeal shall be determined within the Department of Defense. An appeal determined at the highest level provided in the regulations shall be final and not subject to review outside the Department of Defense. A personnel action covered by the regulations is not subject to any other provision of law that provides appellate rights or procedures.

Notwithstanding paragraph (1), a preference eligible referred to in may elect to have an appeal of a personnel action taken against the preference eligible under the regulation determined by the Merit Systems Protection Board instead of having the appeal determined within the Department of Defense. shall apply to any such appeal to the Merit Systems Protection Board.section 7511(a)(1)(B) of title 5Section 7701 of title 5

children.heading: .—Consultation With OPM
children.label: e .—Consultation With OPM
children.text: Regulations under this section shall be prescribed in consultation with the Director of the Office of Personnel Management.
